title: Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata is a variant of Dactylorhiza sambucina, a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is a type of terrestrial orchid. As a variant of Dactylorhiza sambucina, it is classified as a subspecies. The classification of Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata is still a matter of ongoing research and may be subject to change. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, where it is often found growing in wet meadows and along streams.
benefits: Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata is a subspecies of orchid with potential health benefits, although limited research is available. Some studies suggest that Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial and antiviral effects, which could aid in the treatment of infections. Additionally, Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata may help to regulate blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation in the body. However, more research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of this orchid subspecies.
properties: Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata, commonly known as the red helleborine, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Its therapeutic properties are also thought to have a positive effect on the digestive system, helping to alleviate symptoms of bloating and indigestion. The plant's roots and leaves are said to be used in traditional medicine to create remedies for a range of ailments, from skin conditions to infections. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata as a medicinal herb.
constituents: Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata, a subspecies of the orchid Dactylorhiza sambucina, contains various bioactive compounds. These active constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides. The iridoid glycosides, such as sambunigron and sambunigron C, have been identified as contributing to the plant's pharmacological properties. Additionally, the flavonoids present in Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the roles of these active constituents in the plant's medicinal applications.

intro: Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata, also known as the lesser twayblade, is a rare and lesser-known variant of the orchid species Dactylorhiza sambucina. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the lesser twayblade is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried extract, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.
